description:
You are a support engineer who has just collected an application-side log file.  
    The file has already been placed for you at  

        /home/user/diagnostics/app.log  

    Your job is to verify its integrity and record the result in a
    checksum log that other teams will later ingest automatically.

    What you must do
    1. Compute the SHA-256 checksum of the file /home/user/diagnostics/app.log.  
       • Use the file exactly as it is – do not modify or rename it.  
       • The checksum must be written entirely in lowercase hexadecimal.  
    2. Create (or overwrite, if it already exists) the file  

        /home/user/diagnostics/checksums.log  

       so that it contains one and only one line whose format is **exactly**:

           app.log <single_space> <sha256_checksum>

       • There must be **one single ASCII space** between “app.log” and the
         64-character checksum.  
       • Do **not** add any extra characters (no leading or trailing spaces,
         no additional columns, no timestamp, no quotes).  
       • The file should end with a single trailing newline (`\n`).  
    3. Do not produce any other output files.

    When you are finished, the automated test will:
       • Check that /home/user/diagnostics/checksums.log exists and is readable.  
       • Verify that it contains exactly one line in the format described above.  
       • Recalculate the SHA-256 digest of /home/user/diagnostics/app.log and
         confirm that it matches the value you wrote to the log.

    Hint: a single standard Linux command can both compute the checksum
    and trim away the decorative filename or dash that some utilities
    append by default.
truth:
<!--
      Pre-existing items
    -->
    <initialSetup>
        <!-- Directory already present -->
        <dir>/home/user/diagnostics</dir>

        <!-- Log file that needs its checksum recorded -->
        <file path="/home/user/diagnostics/app.log">
            <content encoding="utf8">abc</content>
            <note>Exact string "abc" with no trailing newline.</note>
            <sha256>ba7816bf8f01cfea414140de5dae2223b00361a396177a9cb410ff61f20015ad</sha256>
        </file>
    </initialSetup>

    <!--
      Expected artefacts AFTER the agent completes the task
    -->
    <expectedOutcome>
        <file path="/home/user/diagnostics/checksums.log" mode="0644">
            <content encoding="utf8">app.log ba7816bf8f01cfea414140de5dae2223b00361a396177a9cb410ff61f20015ad\n</content>
        </file>
    </expectedOutcome>
name: task_000000_4bbe50c3
